title: Temperature control for active compound extraction
abstract: A system and a method for extracting one or more active compounds from a cannabis biomass are disclosed. The method comprises obtaining a raw biomass of cannabis plant. A prepared biomass may be obtained from the raw biomass and may be mixed with a solvent to form slurry. The slurry may be heated via a heating unit coupled with an extractor. A temperature at which the one or more active compounds may decarboxylate may be obtained. An amount of heating power required for achieving a safe temperature range may be calculated. The safe temperature may be provided to the heating unit for heating the slurry in the extractor by the heating unit. A spent biomass may be filtered and separated from the solvent and an extract, and the extract may be formulated and sampled.
claims: Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for extracting active compounds from biomass, the method comprising: mixing a cannabis biomass with a solvent to form a slurry; executing a decarboxylation algorithm stored on a heating control unit, wherein execution of the decarboxylation algorithm generates a heating sequence that includes a safe temperature and a duration calculated to avoid decarboxylation of one or more active compounds of the cannabis biomass within the slurry; generating heat within an extractor in accordance with the heating sequence, wherein the slurry is exposed to heat at the safe temperature for the duration, and wherein the heat results in extraction of one or more active compounds from the cannabis biomass within the slurry; and separating the extracted active compounds from a spent biomass within the slurry. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the heating sequence includes one or more preheat parameters, and wherein generating the heat includes preheating the extractor in accordance with the preheat parameters before the slurry is provided to the extractor. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the heating sequence includes one or more extraction heat parameters, and wherein generating the heat includes heating the extractor in accordance with the extraction heat parameters until extraction of the active compounds is confirmed. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein the heat is generated using at least one of microwave, radiofrequency, and steam. 5. The method of claim 1, further comprising identifying the active compounds within the cannabis biomass, and setting the safe temperature based on the identified active compounds. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein generating the heating sequence includes: retrieving data regarding the cannabis biomass, the retrieved data indicative of a temperature at which the active compounds in the cannabis biomass begin to decarboxylate; setting the safe temperature lower than the decarboxylation temperature; and calculating an amount of power required to heat the extractor to the safe temperature. 7. The method of claim 1, further comprising storing the data regarding the cannabis biomass in memory, the stored data including at least one of a cannabinoid content, a cannabinoid profile, and a terpene profile. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein the heating sequence includes one or more cooling parameters. 9. The method of claim 1, further comprising decarboxylating the extracted active compounds after separation from the spent biomass. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more active compounds include delta-9- tetrahydrocannabinolic acid (THCA) and cannabidiolic acid (CBDA). 11. A system for extracting active compounds from biomass, the system comprising: an extractor that receives a slurry formed from a mixture of a cannabis biomass with a solvent; a heating control unit that stores a decarboxylation algorithm executable to generate a heating sequence that includes a safe temperature and a duration calculated to avoid decarboxylation of one or more active compounds of the cannabis biomass within the slurry; a heating unit that generates heat within the extractor in accordance with the heating sequence, wherein the slurry is exposed to heat at the safe temperature for the duration, and wherein the heat results in extraction of one or more active compounds from the cannabis biomass within the slurry; and a filtration unit that separates the extracted active compounds from a spent biomass within the slurry. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein the heating sequence includes one or more preheat parameters, and wherein the heating unit generates the heat to preheat the extractor in accordance with the preheat parameters before the slurry is provided to the extractor. 13. The system of claim 1, wherein the heating sequence includes one or more extraction heat parameters, and wherein generating the heat includes heating the extractor in accordance with the extraction heat parameters until extraction of the active compounds is confirmed. 14. The system of claim 11, wherein the heating unit generates the heat using at least one of microwave, radiofrequency, and steam. 15. The system of claim 11, wherein the heating control unit further identifies the active compounds within the cannabis biomass, and sets the safe temperature based on the identified active compounds. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the heating control unit generates the heating sequence by: retrieving data regarding the cannabis biomass, the retrieved data indicative of a temperature at which the active compounds in the cannabis biomass begin to decarboxylate; setting the safe temperature lower than the decarboxylation temperature; and calculating an amount of power required to heat the extractor to the safe temperature. 17. The system of claim 11, further comprising a database in memory that stores the data regarding the cannabis biomass, the stored data including at least one of a cannabinoid content, a cannabinoid profile, and a terpene profile. 18. The system of claim 11, wherein the heating sequence includes one or more cooling parameters. 19. The system of claim 11, wherein the extracted active compounds are decarboxylated after separation from the spent biomass. 20. The system of claim 11, wherein the one or more active compounds include delta-9- tetrahydrocannabinolic acid (THCA) and cannabidiolic acid (CBDA).
